exampleSentences: Em ơi trời, hôm nay lại quên mang chìa khóa nữa rồi!
Anh vừa nghe tin đó và chỉ biết nói ơi trời thôi.
Chị ơi trời, sao trời lại mưa to đúng lúc mình ra ngoài!
exampleSentencesTranslated: Oh my god, I forgot to bring the keys again today!
I just heard that news and could only say oh my god.
Oh my god, why is it raining heavily right when we go outside!
notes_md:
**“ơi trời”** is like “oh my God” or “oh my gosh.” You say it when you’re surprised, shocked, annoyed, or can’t believe something.

It’s an emotional exclamation, not a full sentence. You can say it alone, or put it at the beginning of a sentence to show your reaction.

It often expresses mild frustration, disbelief, or sudden realization — not usually very serious drama.

Similar: **“trời ơi”** (much more common and natural).  
Stronger: **“trời ơi là trời”** (more dramatic).
notes:
